title: Equestrian statue of Etienne Marcel in Paris France
highlights:
Equestrian statue of Etienne Marcel in Paris France

- Contact

Description: The equestrian statue of Etienne Marcel, with a sword and a scroll in his right hand, stands near the town hall in Paris. The statue was created and started by Antonin Idrac and finished after his death by Laurent Marqueste.

# Statue of Étienne Marcel

## Highlights The equestrian statue of Étienne Marcel, located near the Hôtel de Ville, commemorates the 14th-century Parisian leader who sought to reform royal power, crafted by Antonin Idrac and completed by Laurent Marqueste in 1888.  ...    The equestrian statue of Étienne Marcel, located on the southern side of the City Hall of Paris in the Jardin des Combattants-de-la-Nueve, is a significant monument in Paris. This impressive bronze sculpture pays tribute to Étienne Marcel, a 14th-century provost of the merchants of Paris who  ...    Created by French sculptor Jean-Antoine-Marie Idrac, the statue was completed after his death by Laurent Marqueste and inaugurated on July 14, 1888, symbolizing the lasting influence of Marcel on the city.  ...
